title: TC001-Dashboard loads and shows summary statistics and recent documents
description: Verifies an authenticated user can open the dashboard and see key summary cards and a recent documents section.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login by visiting http://localhost:3001/login (explicit navigate as specified).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type the username into the email field (index 113), type the password into the password field (index 119), then click the 'Sign In' button (index 129).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nawawi@astarahotel.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Assert URL contains '/'
        assert "/" in frame.url, f"Expected '/' to be in URL, got: {frame.url}"
        # Verify 'Statistics' is visible - no matching xpath for 'Statistics' in the available elements, report issue
        raise AssertionError("'Statistics' text not found on the page (no matching xpath in available elements).")
        # Verify 'Recent documents' is visible - no matching xpath for 'Recent documents' in the available elements, report issue
        raise AssertionError("'Recent documents' text not found on the page (no matching xpath in available elements).")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3001
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3001", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type username into the email field (index 39) and password into the password field (index 45), then click the 'Sign In' button (index 55).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('nawawi@astarahotel.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('password123')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click 'My Approvals' in the main navigation (interactive element index 191) to open the Approvals page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/aside/nav/div/a[4]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Delegated' tab in the Approvals area to show delegated approvals, then verify the text 'Delegated' is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/header/div[3]/div/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Delegated to Me' tab (interactive element index 624) to display delegated approvals and then verify the text 'Delegated' is visible.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div/div/main/div/div/div[2]/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Verify URL contains /approvals
        assert "/approvals" in frame.url
        # Verify the 'Delegated' (Delegated to Me) tab is visible
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[1]/div/main/div/div/div[2]/button[3]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
        assert await elem.is_visible()
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
